title: Becoming a Word Detective: Understanding Prefixes
simple_explanation: This page helps us find clues in stories to see if they're about facts or feelings, and how serious they sound. The really cool part is learning about 'prefixes'! These are little bits you stick at the *start* of a word to change its meaning, like adding 'un-' to 'tie' makes it 'untie'. It's like having a secret power to understand words!
detailed_explanation: We also get to discover the power of 'prefixes'. A prefix is a small group of letters that we add to the very *beginning* of a word. When you add a prefix, it changes the meaning of the original word. For example, the prefix 're-' means 'again'. So, if you 're-read' a book, you read it again! Or if you 'co-' (meaning 'join or with') 'operate' with someone, you work together. Knowing prefixes is like having a secret code to unlock the meaning of many new words you might come across. It helps you understand what a word means even if you've never seen it before!

examples_expanded.text: The prefix 'un-' means 'not', so 'unhappy' means 'not happy'.
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of text features and prefixes in a real sentence.

examples_expanded.text: The prefix 'anti-' means 'against' or 'opposite', so 'anti-social' means 'against being social'.
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of text features and prefixes in a real sentence.

examples_expanded.text: The prefix 'tele-' means 'at a distance', so a 'telephone' lets you hear voices from a distance.
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of text features and prefixes in a real sentence.

guided_practice: Identify the meaning of 'prefix' in a sentence. Remember it means: a group of letters added to the beginning of a word to change its meaning.
Match the word 'root word' with a similar concept from your own experience.
Look at the first example again. How would you modify it to make it your own?
application_tasks: Create 3 of your own sentences using the core idea of Text Features and Prefixes.
Imagine you are teaching this topic to a friend. Write down exactly what you would say.
challenge_tasks: Combine the concept of 'prefix' with something you learned in a previous chapter to write a short paragraph.
Can you find an exception to this rule or a creative way to bend it? Explain how.
key_points: Different types of writing (timelines, biographies, autobiographies) have specific features like facts, opinions, and tone.
A prefix is a group of letters added to the start of a word to change its meaning.
Learning common prefixes helps you understand new words and expand your vocabulary.
Creating a personal timeline helps you practice organizing events in chronological order.

vocabulary.definition: a group of letters added to the beginning of a word to change its meaning.
the basic part of a word to which prefixes or suffixes are added.
a serious, official way of writing or speaking.
a relaxed, friendly way of writing or speaking.
a diagram showing events in chronological order.
